name: Nat King Cole
abstract: Nathaniel Adams Coles (March 17, 1919 – February 15, 1965), known professionally as Nat King Cole, was an American singer, jazz pianist, and actor. Cole's career as a jazz and pop vocalist started in the late 1930s and spanned almost three decades where he found success and recorded over 100 songs that became hits on the pop charts.

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Mancuso
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee cites Nat King Cole as an example of an artist with justified record sales and narrates historical anecdotes about him (e.g., Cab Calloway's advice, moving to Hollywood), but does not describe a personal interaction or professional collaboration.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Mancuso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 10, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Luis Conte
about_quotes.evidence: And he loved Nat King Cole, Sinatra, you know, and my sister... She had all these records of all these guys. So I grew up listening to the stuff in the house
about_quotes.doc_title: Luis Conte interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 24, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: I would put on my overcoat and go in there and tinkle and play what I thought I heard on these records by Louie Jordan and Nat King Cole.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Rossano Sportiello
about_quotes.evidence: There is the Fats Waller version, the Art Tatum version, the Bud Powell version, the Nat King Cole version, the Oscar Peterson version.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rossano Sportiello interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, October 2, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Player
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...There has been a number of very famous groups — Nat King Cole. [INTERVIEWEE] That’s true, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: It was inspired by a record that was done by Lester Young, Nat King Cole and Buddy Rich.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
